description:
**Problem**
HLC state must be persisted to prevent stamp regression after restart or wall-clock rollback. Recovery must reconstruct HLC from WAL if SQLite is missing.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §2.8 (write stamps explicit requirements)
- §6.2 (hlc table in SQLite schema)

**Design**
- Add `hlc` table to SQLite schema: (actor_id TEXT PK, last_physical_ms INTEGER, last_logical INTEGER)
- On startup: initialize HLC from max(persisted, system_time)
- On mint: clamp forward jumps to HLC_MAX_FORWARD_DRIFT_MS (default 600s), log anomaly
- On index rebuild: scan WAL records for hlc_max and take max per ActorId
- Persist HLC in same critical section as WAL append/index commit

**Acceptance**
- [ ] HLC state persisted in SQLite hlc table
- [ ] Restart recovers HLC without stamp regression
- [ ] Forward jump clamping logged as anomaly
- [ ] Index rebuild reconstructs HLC from WAL hlc_max

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/index.rs, src/core/time.rs, src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
title: Phase 4: HLC persistence + recovery

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Migration integration suite has an explicit local==remote case-4 test.
- [ ] Forced unrelated-divergence coverage asserts metadata preservation/recomputation, not just state merge and parent choice.
- [ ] Fixture documentation remains accurate about what the corpus covers.
description:
**Problem**
Two design-matrix cases still are not pinned tightly in the migration CLI suite: case 4 where local and remote store refs are equal, and the forced unrelated-history path's migrated metadata/checksum behavior. The related-divergence path now checks merged metadata, but the equal-history and forced unrelated-history cases still leave room for regressions in parent choice, metadata preservation, and checksum recomputation.

**Files:** `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs`, `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store_corpus/README.md`
design:
**Design**
Round out the matrix coverage with explicit equal-history and forced unrelated-history assertions.

- Add an integration case where local and remote `beads/store` are the same OID and assert the expected parent/result behavior.
- Extend the forced unrelated-divergence test to assert migrated `meta.json` behavior and checksum correctness, matching the related-divergence rigor.
- Update fixture notes only if needed to keep the corpus claims truthful.
title: Migration matrix coverage should pin equal-history and forced unrelated metadata

acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] `NodeOptions::in_memory()` runs with no WAL files written on disk.
- [ ] In-memory WAL supports append/flush/rotation and index rebuild paths used in production.
- [ ] Error handling matches disk WAL semantics (same EventWalError variants).
- [ ] Add/extend tests: in-memory WAL roundtrip and replication convergence.
- [ ] All existing tests continue to pass; no regression in disk WAL behavior.
description:
**Problem**
`NodeOptions::in_memory()` still uses the on-disk Event WAL (with `SegmentSyncMode::None`) and only swaps the index to memory. This means tests still hit the filesystem, depend on WAL directory layout, and can’t run as fully in-memory deterministic simulations. It also makes it harder to test failure cases or performance without disk variability.

We need a true in-memory Event WAL backend that uses the same logical paths as the production WAL but stores segments/frames in memory (no fsync, no disk IO). This should integrate cleanly with the existing apply/replay/index pipeline and keep error handling parity with the disk WAL.

**Files:** new in-memory WAL module, updates to WAL plumbing and test harness.
design:
**Design**
- Introduce an Event WAL abstraction (e.g. `EventWalHandle` enum or `EventWalBackend` trait) that covers the methods used by daemon code: `append`, `flush`, `active_segment`, segment snapshot, etc.
- Implement `MemoryEventWal`:
  - Store per-namespace segments in memory (Vec<u8> frames), track SegmentHeader/SegmentId, last offsets, sealed/rotation state.
  - Reuse existing frame/record encoding (`encode_frame`, `SegmentHeader`) so replay logic can be shared.
  - Generate SegmentRow metadata identically to disk WAL to keep index updates identical.
  - Provide read helpers for replay/catch-up (either adapt `rebuild_index`/`catch_up_index` to accept a segment source trait, or add `rebuild_index_from_memory` used in tests).
- Wiring:
  - Allow StoreRuntime/test harness to swap in memory WAL + memory index for in-memory nodes.
  - Ensure no WAL files are created on disk for `NodeOptions::in_memory()`.
- Error parity:
  - Use the same `EventWalError` variants for corrupt frames/headers so callers get the same error payloads.

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/event_wal.rs (abstraction), src/daemon/wal/memory_wal.rs (new), src/daemon/store/runtime.rs, src/test_harness/mod.rs, tests/e2e.rs or new integration tests.
title: Implement in-memory Event WAL backend (no filesystem)
Adapt bd-4rhy span propagation for PR #2 accept loop rewrite
